questions.title: Diabetes Management
questions.text: A client with type 2 diabetes has a blood glucose level of 45 mg/dL and is confused. Which intervention should the nurse implement first?

choices.text: Give orange juice with added sugar

questions.rationale: For conscious clients with hypoglycemia, oral glucose is the first intervention. Orange juice with sugar provides fast-acting carbohydrates to quickly raise blood glucose levels.

questions.title: Postpartum Assessment
questions.text: Which findings are normal during the fourth stage of labor? Select all that apply.
questions.rationale: Normal findings include a firm fundus, moderate lochia rubra, physiologic shaking, and perineal edema. Bladder distention is abnormal and requires intervention.

questions.title: Medication Calculation
questions.text: The healthcare provider orders Heparin 1,000 units/hour IV. The label reads 25,000 units/500 mL. Calculate the mL/hour to be administered.
questions.rationale: Using dimensional analysis: (1,000 units/hour × 500 mL) ÷ 25,000 units = 20 mL/hour

questions.title: Respiratory Assessment
questions.text: A client has crackles in the lung bases. Which position should the nurse place the client in to facilitate breathing?
questions.rationale: Fowler's position uses gravity to help lower diaphragm and expand lungs, facilitating better breathing with crackles.
